info.title: AbsolutePay API
info.description: The AbsolutePay REST API — accept crypto payments, send payouts, run subscriptions, and reconcile, straight from your app. Every request is authenticated with your API key plus an HMAC signature; see the Authentication guide.

value.description: available converted to the quote currency

priced.description: false when no convert quote was available

total.description: sum of priced line values, in the quote currency

expiresIn.description: seconds; clamped to a max of 1h

address.description: permanent, reusable receive address for this network

memo.description: destination tag/memo — required on memo networks; sending without it can lose funds

currencies.description: coins this address accepts on its network

deliveredAmount.description: the amount actually delivered — matters for a PARTIAL settlement

fee.description: network fee deducted on this sub-order

batchId.description: the payout batch number, set once submitted

QuoteRequest.description: specify exactly one of sellAmount / buyAmount

quoteId.description: the quoteId returned by previewConvert (verified live)

merchantPlanNo.description: your reference + idempotency key for the plan

planNo.description: the plan number — pass it as planNo when subscribing a customer

priceNo.description: the price/product number the plan references

merchantSubNo.description: your reference + idempotency key for this subscription

planNo.description: the plan number (from a created subscription plan) to subscribe the customer to

nextCursor.description: echo as ?before for the next page; null on the last page

expiresAt.description: optional link expiry (epoch ms) — past it the link reads EXPIRED and won't accept payment

chain.description: optional — when set, the deposit address is minted up front for this network (invoice flow) and the response is InvoiceWithAddress instead of Invoice

kind.description: which manager owns it; defaults to invoice

InvoiceWithAddress.description: Returned by POST /v1/invoices when `chain` is set — the deposit address minted up front.

address.description: on-chain deposit address to receive payment

memo.description: destination tag/memo — present for memo chains (TON, etc.)

expiresAt.description: invoice link expiry (epoch ms), if set

CheckoutLink.description: Returned by POST /v1/checkouts — the hosted checkout page to send the payer to.

checkoutUrl.description: full hosted checkout URL (when CHECKOUT_BASE_URL is configured)

payPath.description: relative /pay/<token> path (when no base URL is configured)

kind.description: which manager owns it: a billed invoice or a shareable checkout link

customerEmail.description: set if you passed one on create

paused.description: Live/Paused — a paused link won't accept payment

activeDeposit.description: un-expired deposit to resume on reload (same address + countdown)

InvoicePublic.description: The unauthenticated hosted-checkout view — deliberately omits owner-only fields (customerEmail, opens, timestamps).

activeDeposit.description: un-expired deposit to resume on reload

nextCursor.description: echo as ?before for the next page; null on the last page

currency.description: token the payer sends; the picker spans the full catalog

payAmount.description: amount of payCurrency to send so it equals the invoice total

rate.description: 1 when paying the invoice currency; else the conversion rate

payAmount.description: exact amount to send (the order's orderAmount)

memo.description: review note — the rejection reason when status is rejected

BankMaterialsRequest.description: supplementary documents when a bank's status is 'materials'. Each file base64; ≤4MB; jpg/png/pdf.

bankAccountName.description: must match the merchant's verified identity

nextCursor.description: echo as ?before for the next page; null on the last page
echo as ?before for the next page; null on the last page

PageBefore.name: before
PageBefore.description: opaque keyset cursor from a prior page's nextCursor

SearchQuery.name: q
SearchQuery.description: case-insensitive substring search over the list's reference/identifier columns

Unauthorized.description: Missing or invalid API key / scope

get.description: Returns every asset your workspace holds. Each line has an `available` amount (spendable now, e.g. for payouts) and a `locked` amount (reserved against in-flight operations, released when they settle). Read-only; needs the `balances:read` scope.

get.summary: Combined balance in one quote currency
get.description: Values your entire balance in a single `quote` currency (default USDT): a combined `total` plus a per-asset breakdown with each asset's converted `value` and the `rate` used. Ideal for a one-number treasury view across mixed assets.

parameters.name: quote
parameters.description: quote currency for the combined total

get.description: The settled record of your pay-ins, straight from the ledger — the source of truth for accounting. Filter by time range (`from`/`to`, epoch ms) and page with `limit`/`offset`. Reconcile each row against your own order by its `merchantTradeNo`. Needs `ledger:read`.
The settled record of your payouts. Filter by time range and page with `limit`/`offset`; match each suborder on `merchantBatchNo`, its suborder id, and the on-chain `txId`. Needs `ledger:read`.

get.summary: Preview the fee on an order before transacting
get.description: The total fee on a given amount, before you transact.

post.summary: Hosted pay-in order
post.description: Body requires amount, chain, merchantUserId, goodsName (terminalType/expiresIn optional). Response carries prepayId, paymentUrl, qrContent, and the per-order on-chain address.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Retry-safe key — drives merchantTradeNo so a re-POST returns the same order, not a duplicate.

get.description: Fetch a checkout by its `merchantTradeNo` to read its status (`PENDING`/`PROCESS`/`AUDITING` are non-final; `PAID` is settled; `EXPIRED`/`CANCELLED`/`ERROR`/`REJECTED` are terminal failures). Use as the reliable fallback to the `payment.succeeded` webhook — only fulfill once a terminal paid state is reached.

post.summary: Refund a settled order (full or partial)
post.description: Refund a (portion of a) settled checkout order. Confirmation arrives async via the `charge.refunded` webhook, which reverses the accrued fees. Idempotent on refundRequestId.

get.description: Fetch a refund by id and track it from `PENDING` to `REFUND_SUCCESS` (or `REFUND_REJECTED`). The `charge.refunded` webhook fires on success — treat that as the source of truth and process it idempotently on the refund id.

post.summary: Batch payout (idempotent via Idempotency-Key)
post.description: Send a batch of one or more payouts from your `available` balance to external addresses; each recipient becomes a suborder you track independently. Accepted synchronously (`202`) and settled asynchronously — watch the `payout.settled` / `payout.partial` / `payout.failed` webhooks. Always send a unique `Idempotency-Key` so a retried request never pays twice. Needs `payouts:write`.

get.summary: Supported chains + withdraw fee/limits for a currency
get.description: List the chains available for a currency, each with its withdraw fee and min/max amounts. Call this before creating a payout to validate the recipient's chain and show accurate costs.
Fetch a payout batch by `merchantBatchNo`, including each suborder's status — to reconcile a batch that settled partially, or to poll when a webhook is missed.

get.summary: Networks a coin can be deposited on
get.description: The networks available to receive funds, each with the coins it accepts. A balance is one number per coin — a deposit on any network credits the same coin balance, so the network is just the rail the sender uses. Use this to build the deposit network selector.

post.summary: Get a permanent deposit address for a network
post.description: Return your reusable deposit address for the chosen network, creating it on first use. The address is permanent — it never expires, so reuse it for every deposit. Funds received credit your balance for the coin that was sent.

post.summary: Convert quote
post.description: Quote a conversion between two assets: provide `sellCurrency`, `buyCurrency`, and exactly one of `sellAmount` / `buyAmount`. Returns a `quoteId`, the `rate`, and the resulting amounts. Quotes are short-lived — request a fresh one if it expires. Needs `convert:write`.

post.summary: Execute convert
post.description: Execute a previously quoted conversion by its `quoteId`, moving the balance from the sell asset into the buy asset. Re-quote first if the quote has expired. Needs `convert:write`.

post.summary: Define a reusable subscription plan
post.description: Define a recurring billing plan (`amount`, `interval`, `intervalCount`, `totalCycles`) that customers subscribe to. Returns the plan's number; reuse one plan across many subscriptions. Needs `subscriptions:write`.

get.description: List your subscription plans. Needs `subscriptions:read`.

post.summary: Subscribe one of your customers to a plan (returns the authorization link)
post.description: Subscribe a customer to a plan. Returns a hosted URL where the customer authorizes recurring charges; once authorized the subscription becomes active and bills each cycle, firing a `payment.succeeded` webhook per successful deduction. Needs `subscriptions:write`.

get.description: List your subscriptions and their statuses. Needs `subscriptions:read`.
List the charge (deduction) history for a subscription — one entry per billing cycle — to reconcile recurring revenue.

post.summary: Cancel a subscription
post.description: Cancel a subscription so no further cycles are billed. Already-settled charges are unaffected. Needs `subscriptions:write`.

post.summary: Create a checkout — returns the hosted /pay page URL (payer picks the asset)
post.description: Create a shareable hosted checkout link where the payer chooses which asset and chain to pay with. Returns a `token` and a `checkoutUrl` to send the customer to. Settles like any pay-in — fulfill on the `payment.succeeded` webhook. Needs `invoices:write`.

post.summary: Create an invoice / payment link (pass `chain` to mint a deposit address up front)
post.description: Create an invoice for an amount. Pass a `chain` to mint the on-chain deposit address up front (the payer sends funds directly to it); omit `chain` to let the payer pick the network on the hosted page. Returns a `token` (plus `address`/`chain` when minted). Needs `invoices:write`.

201.description: Created — Invoice (no chain) or InvoiceWithAddress (chain given)

get.description: List invoices and checkout links, filterable by `kind` (`invoice` / `checkout`) and `status` (`OPEN` / `PAID` / `EXPIRED` / `VOID`). Page with `limit` and the `before` cursor. `status=PAID` is your settled pay-ins. Needs `invoices:read`.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: narrow to a settlement state (e.g. PAID for the pay-ins feed)

get.summary: Aggregate Checkout stats (active links, opens, conversion, volume)
get.description: Aggregate counts and totals across your invoices and checkout links — handy for a dashboard summary. Needs `invoices:read`.

post.summary: Live/Paused toggle for a checkout link
post.description: Pause (or unpause) an open invoice or link so it temporarily stops accepting payment, without voiding it. Needs `invoices:write`.

post.summary: Void an open invoice
post.description: Void an invoice or link so it can no longer be paid. Terminal — a voided link cannot be reopened. Needs `invoices:write`.

get.summary: Public invoice view for the hosted checkout (unauthenticated, by token)
get.description: Public, unauthenticated endpoint (the `token` is the credential — no API key or signature). Fetch a hosted invoice/link to render your own checkout page.

post.summary: Count a checkout-link view (unauthenticated, best-effort)
post.description: Public: record that the payer opened the hosted invoice page (analytics). Has no effect on payment state.

get.summary: Full pay-in asset catalog — every token × network (unauthenticated)
get.description: Public: list the chains and tokens the payer may use to pay this invoice, so your checkout UI can offer a choice.

post.summary: Live pay-in quote for the chosen token (unauthenticated)
post.description: Public: quote the pay-in for a chosen asset on this invoice — returns the amount and rate the payer will pay. Quotes are short-lived.

post.summary: Mint a deposit address for the chosen asset (unauthenticated)
post.description: Public: mint (or return) the on-chain deposit address for the payer's chosen chain on this invoice, so they can send funds directly.

get.description: Public: poll an invoice/link's pay state. `status` is the settlement state (`OPEN` → `PAID`); `phase` tracks the on-chain step (`awaiting` → `confirming` → `settled`). Use it to drive a live status on your checkout page.
List the countries supported for off-ramp (converting crypto to fiat paid to a bank account). Needs `payouts:write`.
List your registered bank accounts available as off-ramp destinations, with each one's verification status.

post.summary: Register a bank (statement base64; reviewed asynchronously)
post.description: Register a bank account as an off-ramp destination. Returns a `bankAccountId`; verification documents are submitted separately and reviewed before the account can receive funds. Needs `payouts:write`.

delete.description: Remove a registered off-ramp bank account. Needs `payouts:write`.

post.summary: Submit supplementary documents for a bank under review
post.description: Upload the verification documents (multipart) for a registered bank account. The account becomes usable once review passes.

post.summary: SELL quote (crypto → fiat); min 1000 USDT-equivalent
post.description: Quote an off-ramp: provide `cryptoCurrency`, `fiatCurrency`, and `cryptoAmount`. Returns a `quoteToken` with the fiat amount, rate, and fees. Subject to a per-transaction minimum; quotes are short-lived. Needs `payouts:write`.

post.summary: Create an off-ramp withdraw order against a quote + approved bank
post.description: Execute an off-ramp withdrawal against a `quoteToken` to a registered `bankAccountId`. Settles asynchronously and reports via the payout webhooks. Needs `payouts:write`.

get.description: List your off-ramp withdrawal orders and their statuses, for reconciliation.
List the available gift-card templates (designs) you can issue cards from.

post.summary: Issue a prepaid gift card (funded from the workspace balance)
post.description: Issue a gift card. Requires a `title`, a `templateId` (from the templates list), and an `amount`. Returns the created card's number and details to hand to the recipient.

get.description: List the gift cards you have issued.
Fetch a single issued gift card by its card number.

The unified ledger of every movement — pay-ins, payouts, conversions, and fees — in one time-boxed, paginated list. Filter with `from`/`to` (epoch ms) and page with `limit`/`offset`; set `format=csv` for an export. Needs `ledger:read`.
